title: Analyzing a fitted model
text: Every accessor returns tibble, results flow straight dplyr, ggplot2, gt. summary_metrics() gives per-channel ROI, marginal ROI, incremental outcome, contribution share, effectiveness, CPIK prior posterior: accessors follow pattern: Reuse one analyzer() across several calls avoid repeated setup:
code: summary_metrics(mmm)  # Posterior ROI point estimates per channel sm <- summary_metrics(mmm) sm[sm$distribution == "posterior" & sm$metric == "mean", c("channel", "roi", "pct_of_contribution")] response_curves(mmm)      # incremental outcome vs. spend hill_curves(mmm)          # saturation curves adstock_decay(mmm)        # carryover decay expected_vs_actual(mmm)   # model fit over time rhat_summary(mmm)         # convergence diagnostics predictive_accuracy(mmm)  # R-squared, MAPE, wMAPE a <- analyzer(mmm) summary_metrics(a) response_curves(a)

title: Analyzing a fitted model
text: plot_*() returns ggplot object can restyle like : autoplot() plot() select chart type:
code: library(ggplot2)  plot_model_fit(mmm) plot_roi(mmm) plot_contribution(mmm) plot_response_curves(mmm) plot_hill_curves(mmm) plot_adstock_decay(mmm) plot_rhat(mmm)  # Customize freely -- it's just ggplot2 plot_roi(mmm) + labs(title = "Channel ROI, 90% CI") + theme_bw() autoplot(mmm, type = "response") plot(mmm, type = "fit")

title: Analyzing a fitted model
text: rather Meridian’s two-page HTML summary, model_report() escape hatch:

title: Getting started with meridianR
text: Meridian runs Python (TensorFlow / TF-Probability). Install persistent environment: skip , meridianR still works: reticulate provisions environment automatically first time use Meridian function. Use install_meridian(gpu = TRUE) CUDA build install_meridian(backend = "jax") experimental JAX backend.

title: Getting started with meridianR
text: Meridian expects long-format data: one row per geo--time, columns KPI, population, media spend, controls. as_meridian_input() turns data frame Meridian InputData object. spend (impressions), omit media spend used media metric. national (single-geo) model, pass national = TRUE. step--step control, use fluent builder instead:
code: data("sales")  input <- as_meridian_input(   sales,   kpi            = "conversions",   time           = "week",   geo            = "region",   population     = "population",   media_spend    = c("tv_spend", "search_spend"),   media          = c("tv_impressions", "search_clicks"),   media_channels = c("TV", "Search"),   controls       = "price_index" ) input input <- input_data_builder("non_revenue", geo = "region", time = "week") |>   with_kpi(sales, kpi_col = "conversions") |>   with_population(sales) |>   with_media(sales,     media_cols       = c("tv_impressions", "search_clicks"),     media_spend_cols = c("tv_spend", "search_spend"),     media_channels   = c("TV", "Search")) |>   build_input_data()

what: Configure priors and the model specification
title: Getting started with meridianR
text: model_spec() controls adstock/lag, saturation, priors. Meridian’s defaults well chosen, minimal spec often need: Priors KPI scale. conversions non-revenue KPI supply revenue_per_kpi, Meridian places prior channel’s total media contribution default – per-channel calibration needed. can override priors prior_distribution() prior_*() constructors, must match KPI’s scale. particular, ROI prior prior_lognormal(0.2, 0.9) (median ROI ~ 1.2) describes revenue KPI, ROI dollars per dollar. conversions KPI, ROI conversions per dollar – typically far 1 – prior orders magnitude massively -credit media. Either keep default, supply revenue_per_kpi ROI dollar-based, customize scale-free prior adstock decay rate alpha_m (always lives [0, 1]):
code: spec <- model_spec(max_lag = 8, media_effects_dist = "log_normal") spec model_spec(   max_lag = 8,   prior   = prior_distribution(alpha_m = prior_beta(3, 3)) )

title: Getting started with meridianR
text: Create model, draw prior posterior. fit() : Sampling modifies model place (draws stored model’s ArviZ inference_data), sample_prior(), sample_posterior(), fit() return model object.
code: mmm <- meridian_model(input, spec)  fit(mmm, n_chains = 7, n_adapt = 500, n_burnin = 500, n_keep = 1000)  mmm summary(mmm)

title: Getting started with meridianR
Getting started with meridianR

what: R Interface to Google Meridian (Marketing Mix Modeling)
title: R Interface to Google Meridian (Marketing Mix Modeling)
text: R interface Google Meridian, Bayesian Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM) library. meridianR wraps Meridian Python package reticulate, following design keras3: faithful bindings Meridian’s data, model, analysis objects, plus idiomatic R layer tidy data , S3 print()/summary(), pipe-friendly verbs.

title: R Interface to Google Meridian (Marketing Mix Modeling)
text: Install development version GitHub: provision Python side (TensorFlow / TF-Probability) : install_meridian(gpu = TRUE) installs CUDA build; install_meridian(backend = "jax") uses experimental JAX backend. skip step, reticulate provisions environment automatically first use.

title: R Interface to Google Meridian (Marketing Mix Modeling)
text: See vignette("getting-started") full walkthrough.
code: library(meridianR)  # 1. Assemble input data from a long-format data frame input <- as_meridian_input(   sales,   kpi            = "conversions",   time           = "week",   geo            = "region",   population     = "population",   media_spend    = c("tv_spend", "search_spend"),   media_channels = c("TV", "Search"),   controls       = "price_index" )  # 2. Configure priors and the model specification spec <- model_spec(   prior   = prior_distribution(roi_m = prior_lognormal(0.2, 0.9)),   max_lag = 8 )  # 3. Fit mmm <- meridian_model(input, spec) fit(mmm, n_chains = 7, n_keep = 1000)  summary(mmm)

title: R Interface to Google Meridian (Marketing Mix Modeling)
text: Meridian needs Python >= 3.10, meridianR installs r-meridian virtual environment. reticulate can bind one Python per R session, interpreter pinned RETICULATE_PYTHON IDE takes precedence meridianR’s default. another Python initialized first see: fix , point reticulate r-meridian anything else touches Python, restart R: RStudio: Tools -> Global Options -> Python -> Select -> Virtual Environments -> r-meridian (set per project), restart session. fresh session, first line: meridianR warns load time RETICULATE_PYTHON points elsewhere.
code: ERROR: The requested version of Python (...) cannot be used, as another version of Python (...) has already been initialized. reticulate::use_virtualenv("r-meridian", required = TRUE) library(meridianR)

title: R Interface to Google Meridian (Marketing Mix Modeling)
text: Meridian emits warnings Python’s warnings module, normally print raw (file path, line number, ). meridianR reroutes appear ordinary R warnings class meridian_python_warning – cli-formatted silenceable suppressWarnings(). keep raw Python output instead, set options(meridianR.reformat_warnings = FALSE) first use.

title: R Interface to Google Meridian (Marketing Mix Modeling)
text: meridianR early development. current release covers data modeling core — data loading, model specification, priors, MCMC fitting. Analysis (tidy metrics ggplot2 charts) budget optimization roadmap. Everything Meridian remains reachable meridian module handle, low-level escape hatch beneath wrappers.

what: Assemble Meridian input data from a data frame — as_meridian_input
title: Assemble Meridian input data from a data frame — as_meridian_input
text: as_meridian_input() primary, one-call way turn long-format R data frame Meridian InputData object. drives Meridian's DataFrameInputDataBuilder (see input_data_builder()) hood, adding components supply.

title: Assemble Meridian input data from a data frame — as_meridian_input
code: as_meridian_input(   data,   kpi,   time = "time",   geo = "geo",   population = "population",   media_spend = NULL,   media = NULL,   media_channels = NULL,   controls = NULL,   revenue_per_kpi = NULL,   reach = NULL,   frequency = NULL,   rf_spend = NULL,   rf_channels = NULL,   organic_media = NULL,   non_media_treatments = NULL,   kpi_type = c("non_revenue", "revenue"),   national = FALSE )

title: Assemble Meridian input data from a data frame — as_meridian_input
text: data long-format data frame. kpi Name KPI column. time Name time column (dates coerced ISO-8601 strings). geo Name geo column. Ignored (synthesized) national = TRUE. population Name per-geo population column. Synthesized constant national = TRUE absent. media_spend Character vector media spend columns. media Character vector media execution columns (e.g. impressions), aligned media_spend. Defaults media_spend. media_channels Character vector channel labels. Defaults media_spend. controls Character vector control-variable columns. revenue_per_kpi Name revenue-per-KPI column, . reach, frequency, rf_spend Character vectors reach, average-frequency, spend columns reach--frequency channels. rf_channels Channel labels R&F channels. Defaults reach. organic_media Character vector organic-media columns. non_media_treatments Character vector non-media treatment columns. kpi_type "non_revenue" (default) "revenue". national TRUE, treat data national (single geo): geo column constant population synthesized absent.

title: Assemble Meridian input data from a data frame — as_meridian_input
text: meridian_input_data object wrapping Meridian InputData.

title: Assemble Meridian input data from a data frame — as_meridian_input
text: data must long format: one row per geo--time (per time national model), columns KPI, population, media spend, controls. Media, reach/frequency, organic media, non-media treatments optional. supply media_spend media, spend also used media execution metric (spend-based model). Channel labels default spend column names; pass media_channels nicer labels.

title: Assemble Meridian input data from a data frame — as_meridian_input
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ input <- as_meridian_input(   sales,   kpi         = "conversions",   time        = "week",   geo         = "region",   population  = "population",   media_spend = c("tv_spend", "search_spend"),   media       = c("tv_impressions", "search_clicks"),   media_channels = c("TV", "Search"),   controls    = "price_index" ) input } # }

what: Map data columns to Meridian coordinates — coord_to_columns
title: Map data columns to Meridian coordinates — coord_to_columns
text: Thin binding Meridian's CoordToColumns, tells data loader columns hold Meridian coordinate. Scalar fields take single column name; media/control fields take character vectors.

title: Map data columns to Meridian coordinates — coord_to_columns
code: coord_to_columns(   time = "time",   geo = "geo",   kpi = "kpi",   population = "population",   revenue_per_kpi = NULL,   controls = NULL,   media = NULL,   media_spend = NULL,   reach = NULL,   frequency = NULL,   rf_spend = NULL,   non_media_treatments = NULL,   organic_media = NULL,   organic_reach = NULL,   organic_frequency = NULL )

title: Map data columns to Meridian coordinates — coord_to_columns
text: time, geo, kpi, population, revenue_per_kpi Single column names. controls, media, media_spend, reach, frequency, rf_spend, non_media_treatments, organic_media, organic_reach, organic_frequency Character vectors column names (NULL).

title: Map data columns to Meridian coordinates — coord_to_columns
text: meridian_coord_to_columns object.

title: Load Meridian input data from a CSV file or data frame — csv_loader

title: Load Meridian input data from a CSV file or data frame — csv_loader
code: csv_loader(   csv_path,   coord_to_columns,   kpi_type = c("non_revenue", "revenue"),   media_to_channel = NULL,   media_spend_to_channel = NULL,   reach_to_channel = NULL,   frequency_to_channel = NULL,   rf_spend_to_channel = NULL,   organic_reach_to_channel = NULL,   organic_frequency_to_channel = NULL )  data_frame_loader(   data,   coord_to_columns,   kpi_type = c("non_revenue", "revenue"),   media_to_channel = NULL,   media_spend_to_channel = NULL,   reach_to_channel = NULL,   frequency_to_channel = NULL,   rf_spend_to_channel = NULL,   organic_reach_to_channel = NULL,   organic_frequency_to_channel = NULL )

title: Load Meridian input data from a CSV file or data frame — csv_loader
text: csv_path Path CSV file (csv_loader()). coord_to_columns coord_to_columns() mapping. kpi_type "non_revenue" (default) "revenue". media_to_channel, media_spend_to_channel, reach_to_channel, frequency_to_channel, rf_spend_to_channel, organic_reach_to_channel, organic_frequency_to_channel Optional named lists mapping data columns channel names. data data frame (data_frame_loader()).

title: Load Meridian input data from a CSV file or data frame — csv_loader

title: Fit a Meridian model — fit.meridian_model
text: Convenience wrapper draws prior runs posterior MCMC sampling, modifying object place. Equivalent calling sample_prior() followed sample_posterior().

title: Fit a Meridian model — fit.meridian_model
code: # S3 method for class 'meridian_model' fit(   object,   prior_draws = 500,   n_chains = 7,   n_adapt = 500,   n_burnin = 500,   n_keep = 1000,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Fit a Meridian model — fit.meridian_model
text: object meridian_model meridian_model(). prior_draws Number prior draws. n_chains, n_adapt, n_burnin, n_keep Posterior MCMC settings, passed sample_posterior(). seed Optional integer (vector) random seed. ... arguments forwarded sample_posterior().

text: fitted meridian_model, invisibly.

what: Build Meridian input data with a fluent builder — input_data_builder
title: Build Meridian input data with a fluent builder — input_data_builder
text: input_data_builder() creates Meridian DataFrameInputDataBuilder, with_*() verbs add data component one R data frames. Finish chain build_input_data(). flexible, pipe- friendly path; common case, as_meridian_input() wraps whole chain single call.

title: Build Meridian input data with a fluent builder — input_data_builder
code: input_data_builder(   kpi_type = c("non_revenue", "revenue"),   geo = "geo",   time = "time",   media_time = time,   population = "population",   kpi = "kpi",   revenue_per_kpi = "revenue_per_kpi" )  with_kpi(builder, data, kpi_col = NULL, time_col = NULL, geo_col = NULL)  with_population(builder, data, population_col = NULL, geo_col = NULL)  with_controls(builder, data, control_cols, time_col = NULL, geo_col = NULL)  with_revenue_per_kpi(   builder,   data,   revenue_per_kpi_col = NULL,   time_col = NULL,   geo_col = NULL )  with_media(   builder,   data,   media_cols,   media_spend_cols,   media_channels,   time_col = NULL,   geo_col = NULL )  with_reach(   builder,   data,   reach_cols,   frequency_cols,   rf_spend_cols,   rf_channels,   time_col = NULL,   geo_col = NULL )  with_organic_media(   builder,   data,   organic_media_cols,   organic_media_channels = NULL,   media_time_col = NULL,   geo_col = NULL )  with_non_media_treatments(   builder,   data,   non_media_treatment_cols,   time_col = NULL,   geo_col = NULL )  build_input_data(builder)

title: Build Meridian input data with a fluent builder — input_data_builder
text: kpi_type Either "non_revenue" "revenue". geo, time, media_time, population, kpi, revenue_per_kpi Default column names builder uses specific *_col argument supplied verb. builder meridian_input_builder input_data_builder(). data data frame containing referenced columns. kpi_col, time_col, geo_col, population_col, revenue_per_kpi_col, media_time_col Optional column-name overrides; default builder's defaults. control_cols Character vector control-variable column names. media_cols Character vector media execution columns (e.g. impressions clicks). media_spend_cols Character vector media spend columns, aligned media_cols. media_channels Character vector channel names media. reach_cols, frequency_cols, rf_spend_cols Character vectors reach, average-frequency, spend columns reach--frequency channels. rf_channels Character vector channel names R&F channels. organic_media_cols Character vector organic-media execution columns. organic_media_channels Optional channel names organic media. non_media_treatment_cols Character vector non-media treatment columns (e.g. promotions, price changes).

title: Build Meridian input data with a fluent builder — input_data_builder
text: input_data_builder() every with_*() verb return meridian_input_builder. build_input_data() returns meridian_input_data object.

title: Build Meridian input data with a fluent builder — input_data_builder
text: verb returns (modified) builder, calls compose native pipe:
code: input_data_builder("non_revenue") |>   with_kpi(df) |>   with_population(df) |>   with_controls(df, control_cols = c("gqv", "competitor_sales")) |>   with_media(df,     media_cols       = c("tv_impressions", "search_clicks"),     media_spend_cols = c("tv_spend", "search_spend"),     media_channels   = c("TV", "Search")) |>   build_input_data()

what: Install Meridian and its Python dependencies — install_meridian
title: Install Meridian and its Python dependencies — install_meridian
text: Creates (reuses) persistent Python virtual environment installs google-meridian package together backend choice. installed, meridianR use environment automatically future sessions.

title: Install Meridian and its Python dependencies — install_meridian
code: install_meridian(   envname = "r-meridian",   ...,   backend = c("tensorflow", "jax"),   gpu = FALSE,   python_version = ">=3.10",   new_env = identical(envname, "r-meridian") )

title: Install Meridian and its Python dependencies — install_meridian
text: envname Name (path ) virtual environment install . Defaults "r-meridian". ... Additional arguments passed reticulate::py_install(). backend Compute backend provision: "tensorflow" (default) experimental "jax" backend. JAX backend pulls google-meridian[jax] extra recorded default backend future sessions (see use_backend()). gpu Logical; TRUE, install CUDA GPU build (google-meridian[-cuda]). Ignored platforms without CUDA support. python_version Python version constraint used creating new environment. Meridian requires Python >= 3.10. new_env TRUE, existing environment name removed installing. Defaults TRUE default "r-meridian" environment.

title: Install Meridian and its Python dependencies — install_meridian
text: environment name, invisibly.

title: Install Meridian and its Python dependencies — install_meridian
text: skip step, meridianR still works: reticulate::py_require() (set load) provision ephemeral environment containing Meridian first time Python used. install_meridian() recommended want reusable environment, GPU support, experimental JAX backend.

title: Install Meridian and its Python dependencies — install_meridian
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default CPU / TensorFlow install install_meridian()  # GPU build install_meridian(gpu = TRUE)  # Experimental JAX backend install_meridian(backend = "jax") } # }

title: Configure a Meridian model — model_spec
text: model_spec() builds Meridian's ModelSpec, controls adstock/lag structure, saturation, media-effect distribution, prior types, spline knots, calibration. argument left NULL uses Meridian's default. Attach custom priors passing prior_distribution() prior.

title: Configure a Meridian model — model_spec
code: model_spec(   prior = NULL,   media_effects_dist = NULL,   hill_before_adstock = NULL,   max_lag = NULL,   unique_sigma_for_each_geo = NULL,   paid_media_prior_type = NULL,   rf_prior_type = NULL,   knots = NULL,   baseline_geo = NULL,   holdout_id = NULL,   adstock_decay_spec = NULL,   saturation_spec = NULL,   ... )

title: Configure a Meridian model — model_spec
text: prior meridian_prior prior_distribution(). media_effects_dist "log_normal" (Meridian default) "normal". hill_before_adstock Apply Hill saturation adstock? Defaults FALSE Meridian. max_lag Maximum adstock lag, time periods (integer; Meridian default 8). unique_sigma_for_each_geo Estimate separate residual standard deviation per geo? paid_media_prior_type, rf_prior_type paid-media reach--frequency priors parameterized, e.g. "roi", "mroi", "contribution", "coefficient". knots Number spline knots (single integer) vector knot positions, controlling time-varying baseline. baseline_geo Baseline geo, given geo name integer index. holdout_id Optional array/boolean matrix marking held-observations --sample evaluation. adstock_decay_spec Adstock decay function: "geometric" (default) "binomial", named list giving function per channel. saturation_spec Saturation function: "hill" (default), named list per channel. ... arguments forwarded ModelSpec, media_prior_type, roi_calibration_period, organic_media_prior_type, non_media_treatments_prior_type, enable_aks.

what: Prior distributions for a Meridian model — prior_distribution
title: Prior distributions for a Meridian model — prior_distribution
text: prior_distribution() builds Meridian's PriorDistribution, container priors used model_spec(). Supply named priors built prior_*() distribution constructors; anything omit keeps Meridian's default. commonly customized priors ROI priors roi_m (media) roi_rf (reach & frequency).

title: Prior distributions for a Meridian model — prior_distribution
Prior distributions for a Meridian model — prior_distribution
text: ... Named priors, e.g. roi_m = prior_lognormal(0.2, 0.9).

title: Prior distributions for a Meridian model — prior_distribution
text: meridian_prior object.

title: Prior distributions for a Meridian model — prior_distribution
text: Accepted names fields Meridian's PriorDistribution, including: roi_m, roi_rf, mroi_m, mroi_rf, contribution_m, contribution_rf, beta_m, beta_rf, alpha_m (adstock), ec_m, slope_m (Hill saturation), sigma, tau_g_excl_baseline, knot_values.

title: Simulated weekly marketing-mix data — sales
text: simulated geo-level panel used demonstrate meridianR: weekly conversions marketing activity across four regions two years. Conversions generated dominant, structured baseline (level, trend, annual seasonality) plus media effects geometric adstock Hill saturation price control, fitted model recovers good fit realistic media contribution (~10-15%). real data.

Simulated weekly marketing-mix data — sales
text: data frame 416 rows (4 regions x 104 weeks) 9 columns: region Geo identifier: "North", "South", "East", "West". week Week start date (Date). conversions Weekly KPI (conversions). population Region population, constant time. tv_spend TV media spend. tv_impressions TV impressions (media execution metric). search_spend Search media spend. search_clicks Search clicks (media execution metric). price_index Price index control variable.

title: Simulated weekly marketing-mix data — sales
text: Simulated; see data-raw/sales.R.

title: Select the Meridian compute backend — use_backend
meridianR 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development release (data modeling core). First public scaffold package. Python provisioning via reticulate::py_require() install_meridian() helper; use_backend() selects TensorFlow JAX backend. Data interface: as_meridian_input(), fluent input_data_builder() with_*() verbs, faithful coord_to_columns() / csv_loader() / data_frame_loader() bindings. Model configuration: model_spec(), prior_distribution(), prior_*() distribution constructors. Fitting: meridian_model(), sample_prior(), sample_posterior(), fit(), save_model() / load_model(). S3 print() / summary() input data, model specifications, models. Analysis layer: analyzer() plus tidy accessors returning tibbles (summary_metrics(), response_curves(), hill_curves(), adstock_decay(), expected_vs_actual(), rhat_summary(), predictive_accuracy()). ggplot2-native plots (plot_model_fit(), plot_roi(), plot_contribution(), plot_response_curves(), plot_hill_curves(), plot_adstock_decay(), plot_rhat()) autoplot() / plot() dispatch type, plus model_report() Meridian’s built-HTML summary.
